summary: 18th-century Venetian vedute painter, best known for atmospheric views of Venice. Frequently clued alongside Canaletto and Bernardo Bellotto as a vedutista. Appears in quiz bowl almost exclusively as a mention in questions about Venice or Canaletto rather than as his own answerline.

works.name: Vedute of Venice (general)
works.description: Guardi was one of the foremost Venetian vedute painters of the 18th century, painting atmospheric cityscapes of Venice. He is regularly grouped with Canaletto, Bernardo Bellotto, and Luca Carlevarijs as artists who painted scenes on Venice's Grand Canal. His vedute are typically more impressionistic and loosely brushed than Canaletto's precise topographic views.

clues.clue: Venetian vedute painter, grouped with Canaletto, Bellotto, and Luca Carlevarijs
examples: Canaletto hailed from this Italian city, whose other artists included Luca Carlevarijs, Bernardo Bellotto, and Francesco Guardi, all of whom painted scenes on its Grand Canal.
In the 18th century, artists from this city such as Francesco Guardi became renowned for their vedute paintings of its landmarks.

clues.clue: Depicted the bell tower and dome of San Giorgio Maggiore
examples: Francesco Guardi depicted the bell tower and dome of this city's San Giorgio Maggiore in a landscape painting.

clues.clue: Painted nuns in the Church of San Zaccaria
examples: Its painter Francesco Guardi depicted nuns in its Church of San Zaccaria.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Francesco Guardi (1712-1793) was a Venetian painter known for his vedute (topographic city views) and capricci (architectural fantasies). He was less commercially successful than Canaletto during his lifetime but is now appreciated for his freer, more atmospheric brushwork. He appears in quiz bowl primarily as a supporting clue in questions about Venice rather than as a standalone answer.

clues.clue: 18th-century Venetian painter associated with vedute of Venice's landmarks and canals
examples: In the 18th century, artists from this city such as Francesco Guardi became renowned for their vedute paintings of its landmarks.
Francesco Guardi depicted the bell tower and dome of this city's San Giorgio Maggiore in a landscape painting.

comprehensive_summary: Francesco Guardi appears in quiz bowl almost entirely as a mention clue rather than as his own answerline. In the available database, he has zero tossup or bonus answerline appearances, but turns up in four questions (two tossups and two bonuses) as a textual reference. In every case, he appears in questions whose answer is Venice, serving as one of several vedute painters who help identify the city. He is consistently grouped with Canaletto, Bernardo Bellotto, and Luca Carlevarijs as 18th-century Venetian vedutisti who painted views of the Grand Canal and other Venetian landmarks. Specific works mentioned include a landscape of San Giorgio Maggiore (its bell tower and dome) and a depiction of nuns in the Church of San Zaccaria. Players should recognize Guardi as a secondary vedute painter of Venice, distinguishable from Canaletto by his looser, more atmospheric brushwork. He is far more likely to appear as a clue pointing toward Venice or Canaletto than as an answer in his own right.
